channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page.

item.title: Middle east crisis prompts UK NCSC warning on potential Iranian cyber activity
item.description: UK’s NCSC warns of potential Iranian cyberattacks as Middle East tensions rise, urging vigilance from exposed organizations. The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) has warned organizations of a potential incre...

item.title: Fake Google Security site uses PWA app to steal credentials, MFA codes
item.description: A phishing campaign is using a fake Google Account security page to deliver a web-based app capable of stealing one-time passcodes, harvesting cryptocurrency wallet addresses, and proxying attacker traffic through vic...

item.title: Alabama man pleads guilty to hacking, extorting hundreds of women
item.description: A 22-year-old Alabama man pleaded guilty to extortion, cyberstalking, and computer fraud charges after hijacking the social media accounts of hundreds of young women (including minors). [...]

item.title: Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant
item.description: Malicious extensions could hijack the Gemini Live in Chrome feature to spy on users and steal their files. The post Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: How Deepfakes and Injection Attacks Are Breaking Identity Verification
item.description: Deepfakes and injection attacks are targeting identity verification moments, from onboarding to account recovery. Incode explains why enterprises must validate the full session—media, device integrity, and behavior—to...

item.title: Russia-linked APT28 exploited MSHTML zero-day CVE-2026-21513 before patch
item.description: Russia-linked APT28 reportedly exploited MSHTML zero-day CVE-2026-21513 before Microsoft patched it, a high-severity bypass flaw. Akamai reports that Russia-linked APT28 may have exploited CVE-2026-21513 CVSS score of...

item.title: OpenClaw Vulnerability Allowed Websites to Hijack AI Agents
item.description: Malicious websites could open a WebSocket connection to localhost on the OpenClaw gateway port, brute force passwords, and take control of the agent. The post OpenClaw Vulnerability Allowed Websites to Hijack AI Agent...

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude hit by widespread service outage (updated)
item.description: Mar 02, 2026 – 16:50 UTC: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6 – Issue under investigation. Mar 02, 2026 – 15:25 UTC: A fix has been implemented and Anthropic are monitoring the results. Anthropic suffered widespread se...

item.title: Madison Square Garden Data Breach Confirmed Months After Hacker Attack
item.description: The company is one of the many victims of the 2025 Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) hacking campaign. The post Madison Square Garden Data Breach Confirmed Months After Hacker Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access control, exposed keys, and...

item.title: NetQuest launches NetworkLens for hyperscale AI threat detection
item.description: NetQuest has announced NetQuest NetworkLens, a new portfolio of hyperscale real-time network intelligence datasets engineered to power AI-driven cyber threat detection and advanced security analytics. As cyber defense...

item.title: Nick Andersen Appointed Acting Director of CISA
item.description: Madhu Gottumukkala has been assigned to a new role within the Department of Homeland Security. The post Nick Andersen Appointed Acting Director of CISA appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: APT37 combines cloud storage and USB implants to infiltrate air-gapped systems
item.description: North Korea-linked APT 37 used Zoho WorkDrive and USB malware to breach air-gapped networks in the Ruby Jumper campaign. North Korean group ScarCruft (aka APT37, Reaper, and Group123) deployed new tools in a campaign...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm registry. The packag...

item.title: Ukrainian hacker pleads guilty to running OnlyFake AI ID scam site
item.description: Ukrainian citizen Yurii Nazarenko admitted running OnlyFake, an AI-driven site that sold over 10,000 fake IDs worldwide. Ukrainian man Yurii Nazarenko pleaded guilty to operating OnlyFake, an AI-powered site that gene...

item.title: Your dependencies are 278 days out of date and your pipelines aren’t protected
item.description: Applications continue to ship with known weaknesses even as development workflows speed up. A new Datadog State of DevSecOps 2026 report examines how dependency management and pipeline practices are influencing exposu...

item.title: Modernizing with agile SASE: a Cloudflare One blog takeover
item.description: In 2026, agile SASE is the engine for modernization. Discover how Cloudflare One secures humans, devices, and AI agents on a single, programmable connectivity cloud.

item.title: Beyond the blank slate: how Cloudflare accelerates your Zero Trust journey
item.description: Project Helix simplifies and accelerates the onboarding process for Cloudflare One. By using automation and Terraform templates, this tool allows customers to quickly deploy a comprehensive, best-practice configuratio...

item.title: The truly programmable SASE platform
item.description: As the only SASE platform with a native developer stack, we’re giving you the tools to build custom, real-time security logic and integrations directly at the edge.

item.title: CrowdStrike Achieves NCSC CIR Assurance for Incident Response
ShinyHunters leaked the full Odido dataset
item.description: Cybercrime group ShinyHunters leaked the full Odido dataset, the Netherlands is facing the biggest data leak in its history. Odido is a Dutch telecommunications company and one of the largest mobile network operators...

item.title: Claude code abused to steal 150GB in cyberattack on Mexican agencies
item.description: Hackers abused Claude Code to build exploits and steal 150GB of data in a cyberattack targeting Mexican government systems. Hackers abused Anthropic’s Claude Code AI assistant to develop exploits, create custom tools,...

item.title: QuickLens Chrome extension steals crypto, shows ClickFix attack
item.description: A Chrome extension named "QuickLens - Search Screen with Google Lens" has been removed from the Chrome Web Store after it was compromised to push malware and attempt to steal crypto from thousands of users. [...]

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
item.description: HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.

item.title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
item.description: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts.

item.title: Flaw-Finding AI Assistants Face Criticism for Speed, Accuracy
item.description: Using AI to find security vulnerabilities holds significant promise, but the initial products fall short of the needs of enterprises and software developers, say experts.

item.title: Microsoft testing Windows 11 batch file security improvements
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out new Windows 11 Insider Preview builds that improve security and performance during batch file or CMD script execution. [...]

item.title: APT37 hackers use new malware to breach air-gapped networks
item.description: North Korean hackers are deploying newly uncovered tools to move data between internet-connected and air-gapped systems, spread via removable drives, and conduct covert surveillance. [...]

item.title: DoJ Seizes $61 Million in Tether Linked to Pig Butchering Crypto Scams
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) this week announced the seizure of $61 million worth of Tether that were allegedly associated with bogus cryptocurrency schemes known as pig butchering. The confiscated funds were...

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.

item.title: CISA warns that RESURGE malware can be dormant on Ivanti devices
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has released new details about RESURGE, a malicious implant used in zero-day attacks exploiting CVE-2025-0282 to breach Ivanti Connect Secure devices. [...

item.title: Malicious Go Crypto Module Steals Passwords, Deploys Rekoobe Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a malicious Go module that's designed to harvest passwords, create persistent access via SSH, and deliver a Linux backdoor named Rekoobe. The Go module, github[.]com...

item.title: Fake Fedex Email Delivers Donuts!, (Fri, Feb 27th)
item.description: It's Friday, let's have a look at another simple piece of malware to close a busy week! I received a Fedex notification about a delivery. Usually, such emails are simple phishing attacks that redirect you to a fake lo...

item.title: Phishing Attacks Against People Seeking Programming Jobs
item.description: This is new. North Korean hackers are posing as company recruiters, enticing job candidates to participate in coding challenges. When they run the code they are supposed to work on, it installs malware on their system...

item.title: Toxic combinations: when small signals add up to a security incident
item.description: Minor misconfigurations or request anomalies often seem harmless in isolation. But when these small signals converge, they can trigger a security incident known as a toxic combination. Here’s how to spot the signs.

item.title: AWS successfully completed its first surveillance audit for ISO 42001:2023 with no findings
item.description: In November 2024, Amazon Web Services (AWS) was the first major cloud service provider to announce the ISO/IEC 42001 accredited certification for AI services, covering: Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Q Business, Amazon Textra...

item.title: Inside AWS Security Agent: A multi-agent architecture for automated penetration testing
item.description: AI agents have traditionally faced three core limitations: they can’t retain learned information or operate autonomously beyond short periods, and they require constant supervision. AWS addresses these limitations wit...

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Kali Linux + Claude, Chrome Crash Traps, WinRAR Flaws, LockBit & 15+ Stories
item.description: Nothing here looks dramatic at first glance. That’s the point. Many of this week’s threats begin with something ordinary, like an ad, a meeting invite, or a software update. Behind the scenes, the tactics are sharper....

item.title: Microsoft Warns Developers of Fake Next.js Job Repos Delivering In-Memory Malware
item.description: A "coordinated developer-targeting campaign" is using malicious repositories disguised as legitimate Next.js projects and technical assessments to trick victims into executing them and establish persistent access to c...

item.title: Malicious StripeApi NuGet Package Mimicked Official Library and Stole API Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new malicious package discovered on the NuGet Gallery, impersonating a library from financial services firm Stripe in an attempt to target the financial sector. Th...

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20127 Exploited Since 2023 for Admin Access
item.description: A newly disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller (formerly vSmart) and Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly vManage) has come under active exploitation in the wild as part of malicious...

item.title: Flaws in Claude Code Put Developers' Machines at Risk
item.description: The vulnerabilities highlight a big drawback to integrating AI into software development workflows and the potential impact on supply chains.

item.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
item.description: Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.

item.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
item.description: The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.

item.title: Google Disrupts UNC2814 GRIDTIDE Campaign After 53 Breaches Across 42 Countries
item.description: Google on Wednesday disclosed that it worked with industry partners to disrupt the infrastructure of a suspected China-nexus cyber espionage group tracked as UNC2814 that breached at least 53 organizations across 42 c...

item.title: Claude Code Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and API Key Exfiltration
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed multiple security vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Claude Code, an artificial intelligence (AI)-powered coding assistant, that could result in remote code execution and theft of...

item.title: Malicious Next.js Repos Target Developers Via Fake Job Interviews
item.description: Linked to North Korean fake job-recruitment campaigns, the poisoned repositories are aimed at establishing persistent access to infected machines.

item.title: Poisoning AI Training Data
item.description: All it takes to poison AI training data is to create a website: I spent 20 minutes writing an article on my personal website titled “The best tech journalists at eating hot dogs.” Every word is a lie. I claimed (witho...

item.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
item.description: ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2022-20775 Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Path Traversal Vulnerability CVE-2026-20127...

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Guidance for Ongoing Global Exploitation of Cisco SD-WAN Systems
item.description: The purpose of this Alert is to provide resources for organizations with Cisco Software-Defined Wide-Area Networking (SD-WAN) systems, including Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies, to address ongoing ex...

item.title: Manual Processes Are Putting National Security at Risk
item.description: Why automating sensitive data transfers is now a mission-critical priority More than half of national security organizations still rely on manual processes to transfer sensitive data, according to The CYBER360: Defend...

item.title: CISA Confirms Active Exploitation of FileZen CVE-2026-25108 Vulnerability
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed vulnerability in FileZen to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitat...

item.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
item.description: Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.

item.title: Lazarus Group Picks a New Poison: Medusa Ransomware
item.description: The North Korean threat group also leveraged Comebacker backdoor, Blindingcan RAT, and info stealer Infohook in its recent attacks.

item.title: UAC-0050 Targets European Financial Institution With Spoofed Domain and RMS Malware
item.description: A Russia-aligned threat actor has been observed targeting a European financial institution as part of a social engineering attack to likely facilitate intelligence gathering or financial theft, signaling a possible ex...

item.title: Is AI Good for Democracy?
item.description: Politicians fixate on the global race for technological supremacy between US and China. They debate geopolitical implications of chip exports, latest model releases from each country, and military applications of AI....

item.title: Identity Prioritization isn't a Backlog Problem - It's a Risk Math Problem
item.description: Most identity programs still prioritize work the way they prioritize IT tickets: by volume, loudness, or “what failed a control check.” That approach breaks the moment your environment stops being mostly-human and mos...

item.title: Lazarus Group Uses Medusa Ransomware in Middle East and U.S. Healthcare Attacks
item.description: The North Korea-linked Lazarus Group (aka Diamond Sleet and Pompilus) has been observed using Medusa ransomware in an attack targeting an unnamed entity in the Middle East, according to a new report by the Symantec an...

item.title: More Than Dashboards: AI Decisions Must Be Provable
item.description: AI systems have to be able to show a record of what happened and how.

item.title: 600+ FortiGate Devices Hacked by AI-Armed Amateur
item.description: A Russian-speaking hacker used generative AI to compromise the FortiGate firewalls, targeting credentials and backups for possible follow-on ransomware attacks.

item.title: Wormable XMRig Campaign Uses BYOVD Exploit and Time-Based Logic Bomb
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new cryptojacking campaign that uses pirated software bundles as lures to deploy a bespoke XMRig miner program on compromised hosts. "Analysis of the recovered dro...

item.title: On the Security of Password Managers
item.description: Good article on password managers that secretly have a backdoor. New research shows that these claims aren’t true in all cases, particularly when account recovery is in place or password managers are set to share vaul...

item.title: How Exposed Endpoints Increase Risk Across LLM Infrastructure
item.description: As more organizations run their own Large Language Models (LLMs), they are also deploying more internal services and Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) to support those models. Modern security risks are being i...

item.title: Malicious npm Packages Harvest Crypto Keys, CI Secrets, and API Tokens
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed what they say is an active "Shai-Hulud-like" supply chain worm campaign that has leveraged a cluster of at least 19 malicious npm packages to enable credential harvesting and c...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Cartoon
item.description: I like this one . As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Attackers Use New Tool to Scan for React2Shell Exposure
item.description: Researchers say threat actors wielded the sophisticated — and unfortunately named — toolkit to target high-value networks for React2Shell exploitation.

item.title: 'God-Like' Attack Machines: AI Agents Ignore Security Policies
item.description: Microsoft Copilot recently summarized and leaked user emails; but any AI agent will go above and beyond to complete assigned tasks, even breaking through their carefully designed guardrails.

item.title: Lessons From AI Hacking: Every Model, Every Layer Is Risky
item.description: After two years of finding flaws in AI infrastructure, two Wiz researchers advise security pros to worry less about prompt injection and more about vulnerabilities.

item.title: BeyondTrust Flaw Used for Web Shells, Backdoors, and Data Exfiltration
item.description: Threat actors have been observed exploiting a recently disclosed critical security flaw impacting BeyondTrust Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products to conduct a wide range of malicious action...

item.title: VShell and SparkRAT Observed in Exploitation of BeyondTrust Critical Vulnerability (CVE-2026-1731)
item.description: CVE-2026-1731 is an RCE vulnerability in identity platform BeyondTrust. This flaw allows attackers control of systems without login credentials. The post VShell and SparkRAT Observed in Exploitation of BeyondTrust Cri...

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Secretly Installs OpenClaw for Cline Users
item.description: The malicious version of Cline's npm package — 2.3.0 — was downloaded more than 4,000 times before it was removed.

item.title: Best-in-Class 'Starkiller' Phishing Kit Bypasses MFA
item.description: A user-friendly PhaaS tool beats standard methods for detecting phishing attacks by live-proxying legitimate login sites.

item.title: Scam Abuses Gemini Chatbots to Convince People to Buy Fake Crypto
item.description: A convincing presale site for phony "Google Coin" features an AI assistant that engages victims with a slick sales pitch, funneling payment to attackers.

item.title: Critical Grandstream VoIP Bug Highlights SMB Security Blind Spot
item.description: CVE-2026-2329 allows unauthenticated root-level access to SMB phone infrastructure, so attackers can intercept calls, commit toll fraud, and impersonate users.

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2021-22175 GitLab Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability CVE-2026-2...

item.title: Introducing "AI Unlocked: Decoding Prompt Injection," a New Interactive Challenge
Exposing Insider Threats through Data Protection, Identity, and HR Context
Supply Chain Attack Embeds Malware in Android Devices
item.description: Keenadu downloads payloads that hijack browser searches, commit ad fraud, and execute other actions without user knowledge.

item.title: RMM Abuse Explodes as Hackers Ditch Malware
item.description: Remote monitoring and management (RMM) software offers hackers multiple benefits, including stealth, persistence, and operational efficiency.

item.title: ClickFix Attacks Abuses DNS Lookup Command to Deliver ModeloRAT
item.description: ClickFix campaigns have adapted to the latest defenses with a new technique to trick users into infecting their own machines with malware.

item.title: Critical Vulnerabilities in Ivanti EPMM Exploited
item.description: We discuss widespread exploitation of Ivanti EPMM zero-day vulns CVE-2026-1281 and CVE-2026-1340. Attackers are deploying web shells and backdoors. The post Critical Vulnerabilities in Ivanti EPMM Exploited appeared f...

item.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2008-0015 Microsoft Windows Video ActiveX Control Remote Code Execution Vuln...

item.title: Crypto Payments to Human Traffickers Surges 85%
item.description: Chainalysis warns that online fraud is fuelling sophisticated human trafficking operations

item.title: Odido Breach Impacts Millions of Dutch Telco Users
item.description: Dutch telco Odido has revealed a major data breach impacting over six million customers

item.title: Phishing on the Edge of the Web and Mobile Using QR Codes
item.description: We discuss the extensive use of malicious QR codes using URL shorteners, in-app deep links and direct APK downloads to bypass mobile security. The post Phishing on the Edge of the Web and Mobile Using QR Codes appeare...

item.title: Zscaler-SquareX Deal Boosts Zero Trust, Secure Browsing Capabilities
item.description: Zscaler's acquisition of SquareX comes as competitors like CrowdStrike and Palo Alto Networks also invest in secure browser technologies.

item.title: Shedding old code with ecdysis: graceful restarts for Rust services at Cloudflare
item.description: ecdysis is a Rust library enabling zero-downtime upgrades for network services. After five years protecting millions of connections at Cloudflare, it’s now open source.

item.title: Munich Security Conference: Cyber Threats Lead G7 Risk Index, Disinformation Ranks Third
item.description: G7 countries ranked cyber-attacks as the top risk, while BICS members placed cyber threats only as the eighth most pressing risk

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-1731 BeyondTrust Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) OS...

item.title: Fake AI Assistants in Google Chrome Web Store Steal Passwords and Spy on Emails
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of users have downloaded malicious AI extensions masquerading as ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok and others, warn cybersecurity researchers at LayerX

item.title: Introducing Markdown for Agents
item.description: The way content is discovered online is shifting, from traditional search engines to AI agents that need structured data from a Web built for humans. It’s time to consider not just human visitors, but start to treat a...

item.title: World Leaks Ransomware Group Adds Stealthy, Custom Malware ‘RustyRocket’ to Attacks
item.description: Accenture Cybersecurity warns over difficult to detect, “sophisticated toolset” being deployed as part of extortion campaigns

item.title: Nation-State Hackers Embrace Gemini AI for Malicious Campaigns, Google Finds
item.description: Google researchers found that government-backed hackers now use AI throughout the whole attack lifecycle

item.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2024-43468 Microsoft Configuration Manager SQL Injection Vulnerability CVE-2...

item.title: AI Skills Represent Dangerous New Attack Surface, Says TrendAI
item.description: New TrendAI report warns that most security tools can’t protect against attacks on AI skills artifacts

item.title: Time to Exploit Plummets as N-Day Flaws Dominate
item.description: Flashpoint warns of a dramatic drop in the average time between vulnerability disclosure and exploitation

item.title: Nation-State Actors Exploit Notepad++ Supply Chain
item.description: Unit 42 reveals new infrastructure associated with the Notepad++ attack. This expands understanding of threat actor operations and malware delivery. The post Nation-State Actors Exploit Notepad++ Supply Chain appeared...

item.title: North Korean Hackers Use Deepfake Video Calls to Target Crypto Firms
item.description: Campaign combines stolen Telegram accounts, fake Zoom calls and ClickFix attacks to deploy infostealer malware

item.title: Kimwolf Botnet Swamps Anonymity Network I2P
item.description: For the past week, the massive "Internet of Things" (IoT) botnet known as Kimwolf has been disrupting the The Invisible Internet Project (I2P), a decentralized, encrypted communications network designed to anonymize a...

item.title: US Court Hands Crypto Scammer 20 Years in $73m Case
item.description: A federal court has sentenced crypto-scammer Daren Li to 20 years in absentia

item.title: FIRST Forecasts Record-Breaking 50,000+ CVEs in 2026
item.description: This year should break all the records in terms of vulnerability disclosed, reaching or even surpassing 50,000 new CVEs disclosed

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Six Zero Day Vulnerability in February Patch Tuesday
item.description: Six actively exploited zero-day bug have been patched by Microsoft

item.title: Patch Tuesday, February 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today released updates to fix more than 50 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, including patches for a whopping six "zero-day" vulnerabilities that attackers are already explo...

item.title: Phorpiex Phishing Delivers Low-Noise Global Group Ransomware
item.description: High-volume phishing campaign delivers Phorpiex malware via malicious Windows Shortcut files

item.title: “Digital Parasite” Warning as Attackers Favor Stealth for Extortion
item.description: Picus Security warns of the increasingly sophisticated ways malicious activity is staying hidden

item.title: CISA Adds Six Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added six new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-21510 Microsoft Windows Shell Protection Mechanism Failure Vulnerability...

item.title: Poland Energy Sector Cyber Incident Highlights OT and ICS Security Gaps
item.description: The purpose of this Alert is to amplify Poland’s Computer Emergency Response Team (CERT Polska’s) Energy Sector Incident Report published on Jan. 30, 2026, and highlight key mitigations for Energy Sector stakeholders....

item.title: NCSC Issues Warning Over “Severe” Cyber-Attacks Targeting Critical National Infrastructure
item.description: NCSC call firms to ‘act now’ following disruptive malware attacks targeting Polish energy providers

item.title: European Governments Breached in Zero-Day Attacks Targeting Ivanti
item.description: The European Commission and government agencies in Finland and the Netherlands have suffered potentially related breaches

item.title: Inside the Human-AI Feedback Loop Powering CrowdStrike’s Agentic Security
February 2026 Patch Tuesday: Six Zero-Days Among 59 CVEs Patched
New Zero-Click Flaw in Claude Desktop Extensions, Anthropic Declines Fix
item.description: Security researchers from LayerX identified a new flaw in 50 Claude Desktop Extensions that could lead to unauthorized remote code execution

item.title: VoidLink Malware Exhibits Multi-Cloud Capabilities and AI Code
item.description: VoidLink, a Linux-based C2 framework, facilitates credential theft, data exfiltration across clouds

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of February 2, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info Insaat--Fikir Odalari AdminPando A SQL injection vulnerability exists in the login functionality of Fikir Odalari...

item.title: BridgePay Confirms Ransomware Attack, No Card Data Compromised
item.description: The services of Florida-based payments platform BridgePay are offline due to a ransomware attack

item.title: Social Media Platforms Earn Billions from Scam Ads
item.description: Revolut claims social media sites make £3.8bn annually from scam ads targeting European users
